target.name: Strider Technologies

methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Strider Technologies is a Series C-stage defense and security frontier-tech company that has raised $117M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, supplemented by two public benchmarks (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private companies including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a value proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $199M. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company set yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. The peer sample includes Saronic and Chaos Industries at $9.2B and $4.5B respectively (both Series D), alongside earlier-stage peers such as Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI, each valued near $1.4B–$1.5B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $169M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $787M. Stage-progression suggests conservatism relative to peer medians, likely reflecting Strider's earlier stage. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—driven by heterogeneous funding histories and market positioning—and the limited applicability of public comps to early-stage private frontier tech. The median of $787M balances these methods but warrants sensitivity to peer selection and revenue trajectory.
